skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate AI images with FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: FLUX Dev LoRA, FLUX.2 Klein LoRA, Gemini 3 Pro Image, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, Reve, ImagineArt. Capabilities: text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting, LoRA, image editing, upscaling, text rendering. Use for: AI art, product mockups, concept art, social media graphics, marketing visuals, illustrations. Triggers: flux, image generation, ai image, text to image, stable diffusion, generate image, ai art, midjourney alternative, dall-e alternative, text2img, t2i, image generator, ai picture, create image with ai, generative ai, ai illustration, grok image, gemini image
skill.summary: Generate AI images using 50+ models through the inference.sh CLI, including FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and Seedream.
skill.author: tool-belt

security_audit.summary: All static analyzer findings are false positives. The 38 external_commands detections are Markdown code fence backticks documenting CLI usage, not Ruby/shell backtick execution. The 9 hardcoded URLs are documentation links and example placeholders pointing to inference.sh. No weak cryptographic algorithm exists; line 3 is a YAML description field. The skill is a legitimate documentation file for the inference.sh CLI tool with no malicious patterns, data exfiltration, or command injection vectors. Safe for publication.

low_findings.title: External CLI Dependency Required
low_findings.description: This skill requires the inference.sh CLI (infsh) to be installed and authenticated. Users must run infsh login before using any commands. The skill documentation correctly notes this requirement and provides an install link.

content.user_title: Generate AI Images with 50 Plus Models
content.value_statement: Create professional images, product mockups, and marketing visuals using industry-leading AI models without expensive subscriptions. Access FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, and 50 more models through a single command-line interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using 50+ AI models including FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and Seedream
Create product mockups, concept art, and marketing visuals with specific model selection
Edit existing images through inpainting and image-to-image transformation
Apply LoRA customizations for consistent styles with FLUX Dev and FLUX.2 Klein models
Upscale images to higher resolutions using Topaz and other upscaling models
Browse and discover 250+ available apps on the inference.sh platform
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI (infsh) to be installed and authenticated with a valid account
Image generation requires an active internet connection and inference.sh API access
Some models may incur usage costs depending on the inference.sh pricing tier
Output quality and style varies significantly between models and requires experimentation

use_cases.title: Marketing and Social Media Visuals
use_cases.description: Create branded images, social media posts, and marketing materials by selecting the appropriate model for your style and resolution needs.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing professionals and social media managers

use_cases.title: Product Mockup Generation
use_cases.description: Generate professional product photography and mockups using FLUX Dev LoRA for consistent brand aesthetics and studio-quality output.

use_cases.title: Concept Art and Illustration
use_cases.description: Produce concept art, illustrations, and creative visuals using high-quality models like Seedream 4.5 for cinematic 2K-4K output.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a {subject} with {style} style, using the FLUX model. The scene should have {lighting} lighting and a {mood} mood.
prompt_templates.scenario: Create your first AI-generated image with a simple text prompt

prompt_templates.title: Product Photography
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a professional product photo of {product} on a {background} background with {lighting_type} lighting. Use a {aspect_ratio} aspect ratio suitable for e-commerce.
prompt_templates.scenario: Create product mockups for an online store listing

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Model Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate the same prompt "{your_prompt}" using three different models: FLUX Dev LoRA, Gemini 3 Pro, and Seedream 4.5. Compare the output quality and choose the best result for {use_case}.
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare model outputs to find the best fit for your project

prompt_templates.title: LoRA Style Consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a series of images using FLUX Dev LoRA with the prompt "{your_prompt}" and LoRA weight of {weight}. Maintain consistent style across variations for {project_name} branding.
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a consistent visual style across multiple images for a brand campaign

output_examples.input: Professional product photo of a coffee mug with studio lighting on a white background
output: A clean, professional product photograph of a white ceramic coffee mug
Studio-quality lighting with soft shadows and reflections on the mug surface
White seamless background suitable for e-commerce product listing

output_examples.input: Cyberpunk city at night with neon signs, 16:9 aspect ratio
output: A vibrant cyberpunk cityscape scene viewed from street level
Neon signs in multiple colors illuminating wet streets with reflections
Futuristic architecture with holographic advertisements in the background

best_practices: Start with FLUX Dev LoRA for most use cases, then experiment with other models to find the best quality and cost balance
Use descriptive, specific prompts with details about lighting, composition, style, and mood for better results
Run infsh app list --category image to discover all available models before selecting one for your project
anti_patterns: Do not use vague or overly brief prompts like make a picture - always include specific details about subject, style, and composition
Do not assume all models produce the same output - each model has unique strengths and characteristics that affect results
Do not skip the login step - you must authenticate with infsh login before running any image generation commands

faq.question: What is inference.sh and how do I install the CLI?
faq.answer: Inference.sh is a platform providing access to 250+ AI model applications. Install the CLI by visiting the install instructions at the inference.sh website, then authenticate by running infsh login.

faq.question: Which model should I use for text-to-image generation?
faq.answer: FLUX Dev LoRA is recommended for most use cases due to high quality and customization options. For fast generation, try FLUX.2 Klein. For maximum quality, use Seedream 4.5 or Gemini 3 Pro.

faq.question: Does this skill cost money to use?
faq.answer: The skill itself is free, but inference.sh may charge for image generation based on your account tier and the models used. Some models cost more per generation than others.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill supports all three platforms: Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. You need the inference.sh CLI installed on your system regardless of which AI tool you use.

faq.question: How do I upscale a low-resolution image?
faq.answer: Use the Topaz upscaler model by running infsh app run falai/topaz-image-upscaler with the image URL as input. You can also use the image-upscaling skill for more advanced options.

faq.question: What is the difference between the full platform skill and this skill?
faq.answer: This skill focuses specifically on image generation models with curated documentation. The full platform skill includes all 250+ apps across every category including video, audio, text, and more.
